summary: BUG: 3 concurrent installs starve CPU-pod wrapper -> SSE bad-response -> UI freeze. Root cause traced (no install serialization, wrapper.py:1563). Found during MPI-148 live verify.
Added symptom 2: false archive-produced-no-folder (wrapper.py:1424 set-diff, no fallback, extraction actually succeeded). Same concurrency root; folded in. bad-response also seen single-install.

note: picked up — Option A app-side install serialization

Fix A (app serial install queue, downloadService.js) + Fix B(2) (wrapper zip-namelist node-detect, mpi-ci) SHIPPED + logic-proven (serial harness maxActive=1; zip-namelist harness PASS; node --check + py_compile clean). NOT live-verified: needs CPU pod 3-at-once; B(2) needs image rebuild. B(1)/C skipped (moot once A serializes). Stays in doing until live-verify.

App fix committed Cubric-Vision 6e17996 (downloadService.js serial queue). Wrapper node-detect committed mpi-ci 8149575 (shared commit with MPI-196 aria2 split, wrapper 0.2.28). Neither pushed. Awaiting user live-test: multi-model concurrent download on CPU pod (verifies MPI-184 serialize + MPI-196 aria2 tail in one run). B(2) needs image rebuild at 0.2.28 to take effect.

USER-VERIFIED live on CPU pod+RunPod: serialize (1 dl at a time, 3/3), QUEUED feedback (no lone-flash, single dots, fixed Cancel), verify-overlap (next dl during prior verify), cancel-continues-queue. Fix A (app serial queue) fully proven. Fix B(2) (wrapper zip-namelist node-detect) committed in wrapper 0.2.29 — lands on next pod image rebuild. B(1)/C skipped (moot once A serializes). Commits: 6e17996,789035f,87f7849,9fb9b21 (Vision) + 8149575 (mpi-ci).
